argument.title: MOTION FOR DISQUALIFICATION OF HON. JENNY L. McNEILL
argument.standard: A reasonable person, knowing all the circumstances, would harbor a reasonable fear of lack of impartiality. Crampton v Dept of State, 395 Mich 347 (1975).

C1a.factor: Personal bias or prejudice concerning a party
key_facts: Pattern of ruling against father on every motion
Granted Emily's ex-parte motions without hearing
Denied Andrew's motions without stated basis
Hostile demeanor toward pro se father

C1b.factor: Personal knowledge of disputed evidentiary facts
key_facts: Received ex-parte communications through Pamela Rusco
Relied on information not in the record
Made factual findings without evidentiary hearing

appearance.factor: Appearance of impropriety (Canon 2, MI Code of Judicial Conduct)
key_facts: Pattern establishes cumulative bias (Armstrong v Ypsilanti)
Failure to recuse despite documented pattern

authorities: Crampton v Dept of State, 395 Mich 347 (1975) — Objective bias test
Armstrong v Ypsilanti Charter Twp, 248 Mich App 573 (2001) — Pattern establishes bias
In re Dougherty, 429 Mich 81 (1987) — Contemnor must be heard
Canon 2, MI Code of Judicial Conduct — Appearance of impropriety
Rule 2.11, MI Rules of Judicial Conduct — Disqualification required
Liteky v United States, 510 US 540 (1994) — Extrajudicial source doctrine
In re MKK, 286 Mich App 546 (2009) — Cumulative errors require reversal
